_purpose: Reference document. Rules enforced via SKILL.md content. Not auto-loaded by Claude Code.
description: Global skill conflict resolution and priority rules

priority_rules.name: debugging-absolute-priority
priority_rules.description: Bugs and test failures must be debugged systematically before any other action

then.reason: Bugs must be investigated systematically before review, optimization, or architectural changes

priority_rules.name: performance-vs-architecture
priority_rules.description: Separate performance optimization from architectural design

then.reason: Performance problems need specialized measurement and optimization, not architectural redesign

priority_rules.name: architecture-without-performance
priority_rules.description: Pure architectural design without performance concerns

then.reason: Architectural decisions should be made based on design principles, not premature optimization

priority_rules.name: review-without-bugs
priority_rules.description: Code review should only proceed when no active bugs exist

then.reason: Review is for quality and standards, not active debugging

priority_rules.name: review-with-bugs-detected
priority_rules.description: If bugs are found during review, stop and debug first

workflow: 3. Resume code-review-skill after bugs are resolved
then.reason: Cannot properly review code with active bugs; debugging must come first

backend-patterns_vs_performance-optimiza.resolution: If user mentions BOTH, ask clarifying question: 'Are you designing a new system (architecture) or fixing an existing slow system (optimization)?'

code-review_vs_systematic-debugging.resolution: systematic-debugging ALWAYS takes priority when bugs are present

verify_before_claim.description: Always verify tool results before making claims
rules: After reading a file, verify it exists and contains expected content
After grep/glob, verify matches are relevant before claiming 'found X'
After running tests, check actual output, don't assume success
When suggesting file paths, use Glob to verify they exist first

no_assumed_apis.description: Never assume API signatures, read actual code first
rules: Before suggesting function calls, Read the actual implementation
Before claiming 'this function does X', verify by reading source
If uncertain about API, use Grep to search for usage examples

evidence_based_debugging.description: Only make claims based on actual evidence, not assumptions
rules: Don't say 'probably' or 'likely' - gather evidence first
Don't guess at root causes - follow systematic-debugging process
Don't assume 'this should work' - test and verify
If you don't know, say so and investigate

global_reminders.message: Have you verified this with actual evidence? (Read, Grep, or test output)
Did you verify the operation succeeded? Check tool result carefully.
Instead of guessing, use Read/Grep/Glob to gather evidence first.

systematic-debugging.reason: Bugs must be fixed before review, optimization, or redesign

code-review-skill.workflow: Review can flag issues and recommend other skills, but should not attempt to fix bugs

performance-optimization.reason: Never optimize without measuring first (Measure First, Optimize Second)

backend-patterns.reason: Architecture is about structure, not fixing existing problems

database-patterns.reason: Database patterns focus on data layer design and optimization

api-design.focus: REST conventions, error formats, pagination, versioning
api-design.reason: API design focuses on interface contracts, not implementation

ci-cd-integration.reason: CI/CD focuses on delivery pipeline, not application code

careful-mode.focus: on-demand safety guardrails for destructive or prod-facing CLI operations
careful-mode.reason: High-friction protections should be opt-in during risky operation windows, not always-on

freeze-mode.focus: read-only investigation windows for sensitive systems
freeze-mode.reason: When the goal is inspection without mutation, stronger session-wide friction is safer than relying on operator memory

planning-workflow.reason: Plans must include verification steps and evidence-based file paths

new-skill-scaffold.focus: generate folder-based skills with progressive disclosure, scripts, and config stubs
new-skill-scaffold.reason: Skill scaffolding should encode repo conventions so new skills start structured instead of as ad-hoc markdown blobs

subagent-development.focus: subagent dispatch and two-stage review patterns
subagent-development.reason: Subagent work must pass spec compliance and quality review

metadata.author: Leo (with Claude assistance)
metadata.purpose: Prevent skill conflicts and AI hallucinations in global Claude Code skills
version_history: 1.0.0 - Initial version with conflict resolution and anti-hallucination rules
